article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The summary mostly attributes charged claims to the lawsuit and officials (e.g., “accusing,” “alleges”) and avoids adopting the article’s loaded framing as fact. However, it underrepresents industry rebuttals in the summary, creating some slant and negativity bias. The sources_represented field is incorrect, listing only the outlet rather than perspectives.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral, attributed verbs (“filed,” “accusing,” “alleges”) without adopting charged language as fact.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Health links and legal violations are framed as allegations; minimal un-attributed assertions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Avoids presenting subjective judgments in its own voice; qualifiers and attribution are used.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Echoes dramatic phrases only as allegations; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer motives beyond reported allegations.
custom_reasoning.slant: Summary omits industry/trade group rebuttals present in the article, emphasizing the city’s claims.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Opposing perspective and contextual pushback largely absent from the summary.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: No loaded adjectives in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Potentially charged terms (“public health crisis,” “tobacco-industry-like”) are attributed; otherwise neutral.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on harms and allegations without balancing with industry defenses in the summary.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Slight deference to government/legal claims over industry responses.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Language is mostly neutral and attributed, but not fully balanced in perspective.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Sources_represented lists only the outlet; omits city officials, companies/trade groups, researchers featured in the article.
